file.content:
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: spark-pi
  namespace: spark-pi
spec:
  backoffLimit: 0
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: spark-pi
        component: driver
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: spark-pi
      containers:
      - name: spark-submit
        image: apache/spark:3.5.3
        command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - |
          echo "=============================================="
          echo "  SparkPi Job"
          echo "=============================================="
          export SPARK_LOCAL_IP=$(hostname -i)
          /opt/spark/bin/spark-submit \
            --master local[2] \
            --driver-memory 512m \
            --class org.apache.spark.examples.SparkPi \
            /opt/spark/examples/jars/spark-examples_2.12-3.5.3.jar \
            1000
          EXIT_CODE=$?
          echo "SparkPi job finished with exit code: $EXIT_CODE"
          exit $EXIT_CODE
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: 200m
            memory: "512Mi"
          limits:
            cpu: 500m
            memory: 1Gi
      restartPolicy: Never

input.command: kubectl get job spark-pi -n spark-pi -o yaml 2>&1 | grep -E "image:|serviceAccount|memory:|status:" | head -20

content.thinking:
The YAML file already looks correct - it has:
1. Image: `apache/spark:3.5.3` (not `-nonexistent`)
2. `serviceAccountName: spark-pi` (not `spark-nonexistent`)
3. Memory: `"512Mi"` (not `"512"`)
4. RBAC has "pods" in resources

So it seems that either the issues have already been partially fixed before my involvement, or the precondition setup already fixed them. Let me check the current job status since it was running just a minute ago.

Let me wait for the job to complete and then verify.

content.text: The job is running. SparkPi with 1000 iterations takes a bit. Let me poll until it completes.

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 30); do
  STATUS=$(kubectl get job spark-pi -n spark-pi --no-headers 2>&1 | awk '{print $2}')
  echo "$(date '+%H:%M:%S') - Job status: $STATUS"
  if [ "$STATUS" = "Complete" ] || [ "$STATUS" = "Failed" ]; then
    break
  fi
  sleep 10
done
input.description: Poll job status until complete or failed
